messages.content: Okay so Connect officially launches Friday. Feb 28th. All the sprint work and late nights are finally paying off.
That's exciting! How are you feeling about the launch after all the preparation?
Honestly? Cautiously optimistic. The beta feedback was solid and we hit all our internal milestones. But you never know until real users get their hands on it.
What metrics will you be watching most closely in those first few days?

User signups obviously. We're targeting 1,500 in the first week but honestly if we hit 2,000 I'd be thrilled. Also watching engagement - are people actually using the core features or just signing up and bouncing.
Those sound like solid benchmarks. How has leadership been responding to the pre-launch progress?
Actually really well. The VP gave us a shoutout at last week's all-hands which felt good after all those 11pm nights in January. Said the team execution was exemplary or something like that.
That recognition must feel great, especially given how much you've invested in this launch. What's next on your radar?
Well the promotion committee meets in two weeks. March 10th I think. So if Connect performs well out of the gate that should help my case for Director. Timing couldn't be better honestly.
It sounds like everything is aligning well for you. Are you feeling prepared for that committee review?

As prepared as I can be. The Connect launch is my biggest deliverable but I've got the team growth metrics and the Q4 results too. Just need the first week numbers to be strong and I think I'll have a solid story.
Sounds like you've built a compelling case. Here's hoping those first week signups exceed your expectations!

summary: Diane discusses the upcoming MedBridge Connect launch on Feb 28th, expecting strong first-week signups and positive VP feedback. The promotion committee meeting in two weeks could benefit from strong launch metrics.
